class.name: MeshRD
class.brief_description: [Mesh] type for direct GPU buffer access via [RenderingDevice].
class.description: [MeshRD] is a mesh resource that allows direct manipulation of GPU vertex, attribute, and index buffers through [RenderingDevice] RIDs. Unlike [ArrayMesh], which constructs surfaces from CPU-side arrays, [MeshRD] works with pre-existing GPU buffers or creates server-managed storage buffers, making it ideal for GPU-driven rendering workflows such as compute shader–generated geometry. Surfaces can be added in two ways: - [method add_surface]: Attaches externally created [RenderingDevice] buffers. The mesh does not own or serialize these buffers. - [method add_surface_storage]: Creates server-managed buffers that can be serialized and restored. This is useful for persistent meshes whose vertex data is written by compute shaders. [b]Note:[/b] [MeshRD] does not support blend shapes. [b]Note:[/b] [MeshRD] is intended for low-level usage with [RenderingDevice]. For most use cases, use [ArrayMesh] instead.

method.name: add_surface
method.description: Adds a new surface using externally created [RenderingDevice] buffers. The [MeshRD] does not take ownership of [param vertex_buffer], [param attribute_buffer], or [param index_buffer]; the caller is responsible for their lifetime. [param format] specifies the vertex attribute format as a combination of [enum Mesh.ArrayFormat] flags. [param primitive] specifies the primitive type used for rendering (e.g., [constant Mesh.PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES]). [param vertex_count] is the number of vertices in the buffer. [param index_count] is the number of indices. Pass [code]0[/code] if the surface is not indexed. [param aabb] is the axis-aligned bounding box of the surface, used for culling. Surfaces added with this method are not serializable and will not be saved to disk.

method.name: add_surface_storage
method.description: Adds a new surface by creating server-managed GPU buffers. The buffer sizes are determined from [param format], [param vertex_count], and [param index_count]. The buffers are initially empty and can be written to via compute shaders or buffer update methods. [param vertex_buffer_creation_bits], [param attribute_buffer_creation_bits], and [param index_buffer_creation_bits] are passed as [enum RenderingDevice.BufferCreationBits] to control additional buffer usage flags (e.g., enabling storage buffer usage for compute shader access). Surfaces added with this method are serializable and will persist when the resource is saved.

method.name: surface_get_active_index_count
method.description: Returns the number of active indices for the surface at index [param surf_idx]. This may be less than [method surface_get_max_index_count] if [method surface_set_active_range] has been called.

method.name: surface_get_active_vertex_count
method.description: Returns the number of active vertices for the surface at index [param surf_idx]. This may be less than [method surface_get_max_vertex_count] if [method surface_set_active_range] has been called.

method.name: surface_get_attribute_buffer
method.description: Returns the [RenderingDevice] attribute buffer [RID] for the surface at index [param surf_idx].

method.name: surface_get_index_buffer
method.description: Returns the [RenderingDevice] index buffer [RID] for the surface at index [param surf_idx].

method.name: surface_get_indirect_buffer
method.description: Returns the [RenderingDevice] indirect draw buffer [RID] for the surface at index [param surf_idx], or an invalid [RID] if none has been set.

method.name: surface_get_indirect_buffer_offset
method.description: Returns the byte offset into the indirect draw buffer for the surface at index [param surf_idx]. See [method surface_set_indirect_buffer].

method.name: surface_get_max_index_count
method.description: Returns the maximum number of indices the surface at index [param surf_idx] can hold. This is the capacity set when the surface was created.

method.name: surface_get_max_vertex_count
method.description: Returns the maximum number of vertices the surface at index [param surf_idx] can hold. This is the capacity set when the surface was created.

method.name: surface_get_vertex_buffer
method.description: Returns the [RenderingDevice] vertex buffer [RID] for the surface at index [param surf_idx].

method.name: surface_mark_dirty
method.description: Marks the surface at index [param surf_idx] as dirty, forcing the rendering server to re-read its buffer data. Call this after modifying a surface's GPU buffers externally (e.g., via compute shaders) to ensure the changes are reflected in rendering.

method.name: surface_remove
method.description: Removes the surface at the given index from the [MeshRD], shifting surfaces with higher index down by one.

method.name: surface_set_active_range
method.description: Sets the active vertex and index counts for the surface at index [param surf_idx]. This allows rendering only a subset of the surface's buffer capacity without reallocating. [param vertex_count] must not exceed [method surface_get_max_vertex_count], and [param index_count] must not exceed [method surface_get_max_index_count]. This is useful for dynamically sized geometry where a compute shader writes a variable number of vertices/indices each frame.

method.name: surface_set_indirect_buffer
method.description: Sets an indirect draw buffer for the surface at index [param surf_idx]. When set, the rendering server will use indirect drawing commands, reading draw parameters from [param indirect_buffer] at the given byte [param offset]. The indirect buffer should be a [RenderingDevice] buffer containing draw parameters in the format expected by the graphics API (e.g., [code]VkDrawIndexedIndirectCommand[/code] for indexed surfaces or [code]VkDrawIndirectCommand[/code] for non-indexed surfaces). Pass an invalid [RID] to disable indirect drawing for this surface.

members.member: Overrides the [AABB] with a custom one defined by the user. If not empty, this AABB is used for culling instead of the automatically computed one.
